category_title: Active science leaders who connect school content with home-based animal care narratives
scenario_title: Managing a student who assumes a coteacher role
minimal_context: The student is participating in a Lunchtime Science group conversation about a newspaper article.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at this newspaper article about local wildlife and discuss it as a group.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I can lead this part. I'm going to be a wildlife biologist, and I have some facts from my science class to share about this.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's just focus on reading the text first.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Okay, I'll read it out loud for the group and ask the questions.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Everyone, please take out your notebooks and write down one thing you notice in the article.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Okay guys, listen up. You need to take out your notebooks right now and write down one thing you notice, just like the teacher said. Make sure you are paying attention to the article so we can design our experiments.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at this newspaper article about local wildlife and discuss it as a group.

STUDENT: I can lead this part. I'm going to be a wildlife biologist, and I have some facts from my science class to share about this.

TUTOR: Let's just focus on reading the text first.

STUDENT: Okay, I'll read it out loud for the group and ask the questions.

TUTOR: Everyone, please take out your notebooks and write down one thing you notice in the article.

STUDENT: Okay guys, listen up. You need to take out your notebooks right now and write down one thing you notice, just like the teacher said. Make sure you are paying attention to the article so we can design our experiments.
